mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is shifting from goods to services, causing earnings revisions downward and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of resilient consumer demand, offsetting discount rate pressures and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in over 50% of tightening episodes when housing starts rise, or the conditional alpha becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending power, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days without requiring specific thresholds.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates beyond 3.0 percent annualized, it signals an overheating economy that prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances following GDP acceleration above the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates a labor market softening that has not yet forced the central bank to cut rates, creating a stagflationary environment that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of forward return variance, or SPX returns are not significantly lower when both conditions align simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, a rebound in industrial production coupled with declining consumer credit balances indicates a shift from pandemic-era goods hoarding to normal service consumption, which boosts corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive alpha during recovery episodes when the industrial production threshold is breached, or the signal reverses direction in subsequent quarters.
